MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Crosscomplaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Devera L. Petak
Off Calendar Mar-01-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MICHAEL PETRUZZIELLO, Esq. of Greeberg and Ruby Injury Attorneys, APC (Tel: 323-782-0535) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. DEVERA PETAK, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 925-357-3456) for Defendant Forde Mazzola Associates, INC.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FORDE MAZZOLA ASSOCIATES, INC.'s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Crosscomplaint.

Matter discussed. Counsel Petak requests clarification. The Court states the tentative ruling precludes Counsel Petak from serving the cross-complaint filed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant and cross-complainant FORDE MAZZOLA ASSOCIATES, INC.'s ("Forde") Motion for Leave to File First Amended Cross-Complaint is denied.

First, the Petak declaration does not comply with CRC 3.1324(b). Forde does not sufficiently explain why the amendment is necessary and proper and precisely what recently occurred that warrants amendment at this stage of the litigation (trial date-August 26, 2024). Second, Forde mischaracterizes its proposed First Amended Cross-Complaint as a compulsory cross-complaint. (CCP 426.30(a) [forfeiture of compulsory cross-claim].) Forde is actually seeking to file a permissive First Amended Cross-Complaint. (CCP 428.10(b) [permissive cross-complaint].) Therefore, Forde's citation to CCP 426.50 and cases such as Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94 [court shall grant leave to file compulsory cross-complaint in the absence of bad faith] are inapposite. Even Forde apparently realizes that its proposed amended cross-complaint is permissive-and no claim will be forfeited-since it seeks to avoid filing a separate action for indemnity. (Forde's Points & Authorities, 6:14-17.) Third, this motion is untimely. CCP 428.50(b) provides that "[a]ny other cross-complaint may be filed at any time before the court has set a date for trial." In this case, the court set the trial date on June 21, 2023. Forde should have been aware of its indemnity rights against the proposed cross-defendants earlier. Indeed, it filed its cross-complaint against Roes on April 11, 2023, and then sat on the pleading. Fourth, allowing the filing will prejudice plaintiff. Forde, inter alia, seeks to add a new party to this action (Hanson Iron, Inc.). Adding Hanson at this point of the litigation will undoubtedly lead to a trial delay. That prejudice outweighs any claimed prejudice to Forde. (Crocker Nat. Bank v. Emerald (1990) 221 Cal.App.3d 852, 864 [no abuse of discretion denying leave to file untimely permissive cross-complaint].

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)
